data.narrative:
Fire Point is a Series A–stage defense and security company founded by Denys Shtilerman, Iryna Terekh, and Yehor Skalyha, with the Ukrainian Ministry of Defense as an anchor backer. Valuation assessment draws from a universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI.

The peer-median method, applied across four same-sector, same-stage private comparables, yields a central point of $225M. The peer set spans $125M (NODA AI) to $500M (Stark Defence), with Vatn Systems at $300M and Ares Industries at $150M. This range reflects material heterogeneity within early-stage defense-tech valuations. No revenue multiples, DCF sensitivities, or transaction precedents were available to triangulate against the peer median, limiting the analysis to a single methodology.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $191M (low) to $259M (high), centered at $225M. This positioning reflects Fire Point's alignment with peer-stage and sector benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue profile and growth trajectory relative to named comparables, the geopolitical risk premium appropriate for a Ukrainian-domiciled firm, and whether the Ukrainian MoD relationship commands a strategic discount or premium. A second valuation method—revenue multiple or precedent transaction analysis—would strengthen conviction in this range.
